pattern: Sort descending and greedily pick elements from the front until the running sum exceeds the sum of what remains.
hints: The subsequence sum must strictly exceed the sum of elements not chosen. Think about how sorting by descending value lets you take the fewest elements.
Compute the total sum first, then iterate through the sorted-descending array; stop the moment your accumulated sum surpasses total minus accumulated.
Because the answer is unique and must be non-increasing, sorting descending and picking greedily from the left gives you the correct subsequence directly — no backtracking needed.

edge_inputs.description: single element — trivially the whole array
two equal elements — need both since sum must be strictly greater
one large element dominates the rest
all elements equal — need ceil(n/2)+1 elements
example from statement: multiple candidates, greedily take fewest largest
ties in value — must take enough to strictly exceed remainder
increasing order input — requires full sort before greedy
maximum values, minimum length — one element suffices

stress.description: 500 random integers in [1, 100] — exercises greedy across many configurations
